raw_consumer_statement:
I am the co-owner of XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX, a fine wine retailer in XXXX, Florida. I am filing this complaint against XXXX XXXXXXXX. for an unjustified chargeback and pre-arbitration process that resulted in a financial loss of {$6400.00} to my business. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, an individual used a Citibank XXXX ending in XXXX to purchase a {$5800.00} bottle of wine from our website. We verified the billing address, ZIP code, and CVV through our processor Authorize.net all matched and the transaction was fully approved ( Transaction ID : XXXX ). We fulfilled the order in good faith. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank initiated a chargeback claiming unauthorized use. We submitted all documentation. The dispute was adjudicated in our favor and funds were returned. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank 's agent XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Response ) issued a pre-arbitration notice requiring acceptance or automatic fund deduction. We accepted in good faith. Subsequently, {$5800.00} was re-deducted from our account and a {$620.00} fee was imposed for a proceeding we were never notified of, invited to, or represented in. 
We bring three specific failures to the CFPB 's attention : Citibank publicly advertises fraud protection on its own website yet transferred the entire financial loss to an innocent, compliant merchant instead of honoring that guarantee.

Citibank routinely sends real-time Citi ID Check verification prompts and SMS fraud alerts for transactions far smaller than {$5800.00} I receive these myself as a Citibank cardholder . Citibank either failed to deploy these tools for this transaction or the cardholder ignored the alert. Either way, a compliant merchant bears the loss.

The pre-arbitration proceeding was conducted entirely without our participation, denying us basic procedural fairness before {$6400.00} was extracted from our account.

We request the CFPB investigate Citibank N.A. 's chargeback and pre-arbitration practices and require full reimbursement of {$6400.00}.

Attachments : Exhibits A, B, D, E, F, G, H, I
